reviews_list.batch_info: B.Tech. in Mechanical Engineering - Batch of 2019
reviews_list.review_title: Our college provides good infrastructure and facilities.

review_content.Placements: Students get placed in our college. The salary is not offered to the students. Companies do not recruit students from our college. Roles are not offered to the students.
review_content.Infrastructure: Facilities available in our college are good. All type of infrastructure is available in our course. Quality of the facilities available in our hostel is good. Quality of the food available in the mess and canteen is good. Medical facilities are good. Sports and games are also available here.
review_content.Faculty: Teachers in our college are helpful, qualified and knowledgeable. Their teaching quality is extraordinary. The course curriculum is relevant, and it makes the students industry-ready.

reviews_list.batch_info: B.Tech. in Electrical and Electronics Engineering - Batch of 2018
reviews_list.review_title: Really good infrastructure and facilities.

review_content.Placements: Around 60% of the students got placed. The lowest, average and highest salaries offered are Rs. 10,000, Rs. 15,000 and Rs. 20,000 respectively. Prime Focus is the top recruiting company, and it's an MNC company. The rules are that students are not allowed to use mobile phones in the college. But, I am from Bhupalapally, and I have to take mobile and go to college. Around 70% of the students got internships.
review_content.Infrastructure: In terms of infrastructure, VBIT is one of the best colleges in the country. Air-conditioned classrooms with Wi-Fi enabled LCD projectors, tutorial rooms, centrally air-conditioned library, etc., are available. Classrooms at VBIT facilitate a blend of internet, printing and scanning facilities. Free Wi-Fi is available on the campus. We have 2 canteens providing quality food. Medical facilities are good, but the college is poor in sports and games.

faqs.answer: A:    Yes, Omega PG College reserves 30% of the total MBA seats under the Management Quota. These seats are allotted to students who fulfil the preset eligibility criteria. The college releases a merit list of shortlisted applicants on its official website and the selected candidates are also informed individually. The admissions under this category are to be ratified by Telangana State Council for Higher Education and Osmania University, Hyderabad.
faqs.question: Is there any management quota for MBA admission at Omega PG College?

faqs.answer: A:    The total seat intake for MBA at Omega PG College is of 240 students. It is further divided into Category A and Category B. 70% of  the total seats are reserved under Category A. These seats are allotted by the TSICET Convener. Remaining 30% of the seats are reserved under Category B (Management seats). The mentioned information has been sourced from the college's official website. It is still subject to change and hence, is indicative.
faqs.question: What is the total seat intake for MBA at Omega PG College?

faqs.answer: A:    MBA admission at Omega PG College is primarily based on TS ICET score, followed by TS ICET counselling. However, this is the selection criteria for 70% of the total MBA seats available at the college. For the remaining 30% seats, Omega PG College provides admissions based on the applicant's merit in UG/Bachelor's degree. Such applicants can directly apply at the college for admission (under the Management Quota).
faqs.question: Can I get admission to MBA at Omega PG College without TS ICET?

faqs.answer: A:    Omega PG College MBA admission is provided through TS ICET counselling. Candidates need to get their documents verified by the TSCHE officials to proceed with the participation in the TS ICET counselling process. The documents required during this process are as follows:TS ICET 2024 rank cardTS ICET hall ticket 2024Aadhaar CardClass 12 or equivalent marks memo/mark sheetClass 10 or its equivalent memo-cum-pass certificateDegree memorandum of marksDegree provisional pass certificateStudy/Bonafide Certificate from Class 9 to DegreeTransfer Certificate (TC)Caste Certificate of SC/ST/BC/Minorities (if applicable)Income Certificate issued by MRO (on or after January 1, 2024)PH/NCC/CAP/Sports and Games Certificates (if applicable)Residence Certificate for a period of seven years preceding the year of the qualifying examEmployer Certificate (If applicable)

overview_faqs.question: What is the TSICET application fee?
overview_faqs.answer: Candidates applying for the TSICET 2024 exam must pay INR 750 or INR 550. The charge varies on the candidate's category. Unreserved category candidates are need to pay INR 750. While reserved category candidates must pay INR 550 for the TSICET application fee. SC, ST, and differently abled are among the reserved categories that receive fee concessions. Candidates claiming a fee concession must upload the applicable category certificate; otherwise, the form will be rejected in further stages of the examination process.

For the General category, the Nikam Institute of Technology (Polytechnic) B.E. / B.Tech cutoff 2025 ranged between 3.55 and 87.41, with the most sought-after branch for admission being Computer Science, which had the highest cutoff percentile of 87.41. Nikam Institute of Technology (Polytechnic) MHT CET cutoff 2025 was released as percentiles, which means the higher the cutoff percentile, the more competitive the course is for admission and vice versa. faqs.answer: A.C.T. College of Engineering & Technology has below criteria for taking admissions to it's various courses: CoursesEligibilityB.E. / B.TechCandidate must have passed VIII, IX, X, XI & XII Std. from schools situated in Tamil Nadu. Tamil Nadu native candidate who have passed any of the VIII, IX, X, XI & XII Std. or their equivalent exam from schools situated outside Tamil Nadu can also apply. They should produce Original Nativity Certificate. Children of Central Government Employees, irrespective of their nativity, provided their parents or guardians (only if both parents are not alive) have been serving in Tamil Nadu continuously for the past five years can be eligible to apply. Sons & daughters of All India service Tamil Nadu cadre officers are eligible to apply. A Self declaration from the parent in this regard is to be produced. Children of employees in the Public Sector or Government recognised institutions had been working in their organisations for the past five years are also eligible, irrespective of their domicile. Other State candidates who have studied VIII, IX, X, XI and XII Std. in Tamil Nadu are eligible to apply.
